text:
The inductive step of an inductive proof shows that for
$k \ge 0$
, if
$\sum\limits_{j=0}^k 2^j = 2^{k+1} -1$
, then
$\sum\limits_{j=0}^{k+1} 2^j = 2^{k+2} -1$
. Which step of the proof uses the inductive hypothesis?
$$ egin{array}{lll}
\sum\limits_{j=0}^{k+1} 2^j &  =  \sum\limits_{j=0}^{k} 2^j  + 2^{k+1}& ~(\mbox{Step}~1)\
\sum\limits_{j=0}^{k+1} 2^j & =  (2^{k+1} - 1 )  + 2^{k+1}  &~(\mbox{Step}~2)\
\sum\limits_{j=0}^{k+1} 2^j & =  2 \cdot 2^{k+1} - 1 & ~(\mbox{Step}~3)\
\sum\limits_{j=0}^{k+1} 2^j & =  2^{k+2} - 1  &~(\mbox{Step}~4)
\end{array}$$

The inductive step of an inductive proof shows that for
$k \ge 4$
, if
$2^{k} \ge 3k$
, then
$2^{k+1} \ge 3(k+1)$
.  Which step of the proof uses the inductive hypothesis?
$$ egin{array}{lll}
2^{k+1}  & \ge  2 \cdot 2^k &~(\mbox{Step}~1)\
2^{k+1} & \ge   2 \cdot 3k  &~(\mbox{Step}~2)\
2^{k+1} & \ge  3k + 3k  &~(\mbox{Step}~3)\
2^{k+1} & \ge   3k + 3  &~(\mbox{Step}~4)\
2^{k+1} & \ge   3(k+1)  &~(\mbox{Step}~5)
\end{array}$$

The inductive step of an inductive proof shows that for
$k \ge 4$
, if
$2^k \ge 3k$
, then
$2^{k+1} \ge 3(k+1)$
. Which step uses the fact that
$k \ge 4 \ge 1$
?
$$ egin{array}{lll}
2^{k+1}  & \ge  2 \cdot 2^k & ~(\mbox{Step}~1)\
2^{k+1}  & \ge  2 \cdot 3k   &~(\mbox{Step}~2)\
2^{k+1}  & \ge 3k + 3k & ~(\mbox{Step}~3)\
2^{k+1}  & \ge 3k + 3  &~(\mbox{Step}~4)\
2^{k+1}  & \ge 3(k+1)  &~(\mbox{Step}~5)
\end{array}$$

The inductive step of an inductive proof shows that for
$k \ge 4$
, if
$k! \ge 2^k$
, then
$(k+1)! \ge 2^{k+1}$
.
However the lines are not labeled with the proper justification.
Which  line should be labeled "Inductive Hypothesis"?
$$ egin{array}{lll}
(k+1)! &  \ge (k+1) \cdot k \cdot (k-1) \cdots 2 \cdot 1 &~(\mbox{Step}~1)\
(k+1)! & \ge   (k+1) \cdot k!  &~(\mbox{Step}~2)\
(k+1)! & \ge  (k+1) \cdot 2^k  &~(\mbox{Step}~3)\
(k+1)! & \ge  2 \cdot 2^k  &~(\mbox{Step}~4)\
(k+1)! & \ge  2^{k+1}  &~(\mbox{Step}~5)
\end{array}$$

The inductive step of an inductive proof shows that for
$k \ge 4$
, if
$k! \ge 2^k$
, then
$(k+1)! \ge 2^{k+1}$
.
However the lines are not labeled with the proper justification.
Which  line should be labeled "Because
$k \ge 4 \ge 1$
"?
$$ egin{array}{lll}
(k+1)!  & \ge (k+1) \cdot k \cdot (k-1) \cdots 2 \cdot 1 &~(\mbox{Step}~1)\
(k+1)!  & \ge   (k+1) \cdot k!  &~(\mbox{Step}~2)\
(k+1)!  & \ge  (k+1) \cdot 2^k  &~(\mbox{Step}~3)\
(k+1)!  & \ge  2 \cdot 2^k  &~(\mbox{Step}~4)\
(k+1)!  & \ge  2^{k+1}  &~(\mbox{Step}~5)
\end{array}$$

The inductive step of an inductive proof shows that for
$k \ge 1$
, if
$\sum\limits_{j=1}^k (6j+1) = k(3k+4)$
, then
$\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1} (6j+1) = (k+1)(3(k+1)+4)$
.
Which step of the proof uses the inductive hypothesis?
$$ egin{array}{lll}
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1} (6j+1)  & =  \sum\limits_{j=1}^{k} (6j+1)  + (6(k+1)+1) & ~(\mbox{Step}~1)\
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1} (6j+1)  & =   k(3k+4)  + (6(k+1)+1)  &~(\mbox{Step}~2)\
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1} (6j+1)  & =  3 k^2 + 4k + 6k + 7 & ~(\mbox{Step}~3)\
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1} (6j+1)  & =  3(k+1)^2 + 4(k+1) & ~(\mbox{Step}~4)\
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1} (6j+1)  & =  (k+1)(3(k+1)+4)  &~(\mbox{Step}~5)
\end{array}$$

The inductive step of an inductive proof shows that for
$k \ge 1$
, if
$\sum\limits_{j=1}^k j(j+1) = rac 1 3 k(k+1)(k+2)$
, then
$\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1} j(j+1) =  rac 1 3  (k+1)(k+2)(k+3)$
.
Parts of the proof are shown below. Select the expression that should replace the
$[?]$
.
$$ egin{array}{lll}
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1}  j(j+1)  & =  \left[?ight] & ~(\mbox{Step}~1)\
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1}  j(j+1)  & =    rac 1 3 k(k+1)(k+2)  + (k+1)(k+2)  &~\mbox{By the inductive hypothesis}\
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1}  j(j+1)  & =  \cdots  & ~(\mbox{Algebraic Steps})\
\sum\limits_{j=1}^{k+1}  j(j+1)  & =   rac 1 3  (k+1)(k+2)(k+3)
\end{array}$$
